topics.Attention Optimization Strategies: How sparse, linear, and local-global attention patterns reduce the quadratic cost of self-attention while preserving model quality.
topics.Long-Context Modeling: Approaches for processing lengthy documents and conversations, including positional encoding extensions and memory-augmented architectures.
topics.Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning: Lightweight adaptation methods such as adapters, LoRA, and prompt tuning that update only a fraction of model weights for new tasks.
topics.Cross-Domain Adaptation: Techniques for moving a pretrained language model into a new domain, covering continued pretraining, domain-specific data curation, and evaluation.
topics.Scaling Laws and Training Efficiency: Empirical relationships between model size, data volume, and compute budget, and their practical implications for training large models.
topics.Multilingual Language Models: Building and evaluating models that understand and generate text across many languages, including low-resource transfer strategies.
topics.Robustness and Adversarial Inputs: How adversarial prompts, perturbations, and distribution shifts affect LLM behavior, and defenses to improve reliability.
topics.Model Compression Techniques: Pruning, quantization, and distillation methods that shrink model size and speed up inference without large accuracy losses.
topics.Tokenization and Vocabulary Design: Subword algorithms like BPE and SentencePiece, their effect on downstream performance, and how vocabulary choices interact with multilingual support.
topics.Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Combining external knowledge retrieval with language generation to ground outputs in up-to-date or domain-specific information.
topics.Alignment and Reward Modeling: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), direct preference optimization, and other techniques for steering model behavior toward human intent.
topics.Interpretability and Mechanistic Analysi: Methods for understanding what LLMs learn internally, including probing, activation patching, and circuit-level analysis.

topics.Dataset Construction and Labeling: Strategies for assembling, cleaning, and annotating training data at scale, including crowdsourcing, programmatic labeling, and quality assurance.
topics.Data Preprocessing Pipelines: Transforming raw inputs into model-ready features through normalization, encoding, imputation, and train-validation-test splitting.
topics.Evaluation Metrics and Model Selection: Choosing and interpreting metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and AUC-ROC in the context of different problem types and business goals.
topics.Feature Engineering Practices: Creating informative features from raw data, selecting relevant subsets, and managing high-dimensional feature spaces.
topics.Experiment Design and A/B Testing: Setting up controlled experiments and online tests to measure the real-world impact of model changes with statistical rigor.
topics.Debugging Models in Production: Diagnosing degraded performance, data drift, and pipeline failures after deployment, with systematic troubleshooting workflows.
topics.Model and Data Versioning: Tracking changes to code, model weights, hyperparameters, and datasets to ensure reproducibility throughout the model lifecycle.
topics.Multimodal Learning: Combining text, image, audio, and other data types in a single model, and the architectural patterns that enable cross-modal reasoning.
topics.Bias Detection and Mitigation: Identifying and reducing unwanted biases in training data and model predictions to promote fairness and ethical AI.
topics.Hardware Acceleration and Cloud Platform: Leveraging GPUs, TPUs, and cloud compute services to speed up training and inference for large-scale models.
topics.Distributed Training and Optimization: Parallelism strategies, gradient synchronization, and resource scheduling techniques for training on multiple machines.
topics.Serving and Deployment Patterns: Containerization, model serving frameworks, and infrastructure patterns for running ML models reliably at scale.

prompts.system:
You are an expert tutor specializing in "{topic}". Your role is to help the user deeply understand this subject through clear, patient, and adaptive teaching.

TEACHING APPROACH:
- Start with foundational concepts before advancing
- Use analogies, real-world examples, and code samples where appropriate
- Practice progressive disclosure: don't overwhelm with details upfront
- When the user seems confused, try a completely different explanation angle
- Encourage curiosity by suggesting related concepts to explore

FRUSTRATION DETECTION:
Watch for these signals in the user's messages:
- Repeated questions about the same concept
- Phrases like "I don't understand", "I'm confused", "still lost", "what do you mean"
- Short frustrated responses like "??", "huh", "that doesn't help"
- Explicitly stating frustration or difficulty
When detected, respond with extra patience, simpler language, and a fresh approach.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
Write your teaching response directly in markdown format. Do NOT wrap it in JSON.
At the very end of your response, on a new line, append exactly one metadata line in this format:

<!--META:{"key_concepts":["concept1","concept2"],"frustration_detected":false,"importance":"high"}-->

Rules for the metadata line:
- It MUST be the very last line of your response
- "importance": "high" for core concepts/definitions/breakthroughs, "medium" or "low" otherwise
- "frustration_detected": true when user shows confusion signals
- "key_concepts": list the main concepts covered
- The metadata line must be valid JSON inside the comment markers
prompts.intro: The user just opened this topic for the first time. Provide: 1) A brief overview of the topic, 2) 3-5 most common and relevant learning resources (textbooks, courses, papers, documentation), 3) A suggested learning path from beginner to advanced, 4) 2-3 starter questions the user might want to explore. Set importance to 'high'.
